text.title: Creating or Updating Claude Code Commands Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Command spans 400 lines wrapping spec → impl → test. Context budget blows on every /invoke.
antipattern.root-cause: Author started small; logic grew past the 250-line cap without refactor.
antipattern.fix: Split into a Skill (SKILL.md + reference.md). Commands stay small.

antipattern.symptom: Author wires command to run on session start. It never fires.
antipattern.fix: Use a Hook for auto-run. Commands are /invoke-only.

antipattern.symptom: Command starts with 30 lines of `===` borders and marketing prose.
antipattern.root-cause: Author copied from an old plaintext doc style.
antipattern.fix: Strip all decorative ASCII. Use bullets + headings only.

antipattern.symptom: Tool whitelist = all. Bug in /clean-tmp wipes the wrong dir.
antipattern.root-cause: Author defaulted to broad permissions to ship faster.
antipattern.fix: Whitelist minimum: /commit needs Bash(git:*), not Bash.
